title: Understanding Tangential Speed
text: Tangential speed is the linear speed of a point on the rim of a rotating object. It can be calculated using the formula: tangential speed $$=$$ angular speed * radius.

title: Calculate Angular Speed
text: To find the tangential speed, we first need to calculate the angular speed at the end of the 10-second interval. Use the formula: angular speed $$=$$ initial angular speed + (angular acceleration * time).

title: Calculate Angular Speed
text: Given that the initial angular speed is $$0$$ $$\frac{rad}{s}$$, angular acceleration is $$4$$ $$\frac{rad}{s^2}$$, and time is $$10$$ s, calculate the angular speed.

title: Calculate Tangential Speed
text: Now that you have the angular speed, use it to find the tangential speed using the formula: tangential speed $$=$$ angular speed * radius.

title: Calculate Tangential Speed
text: With an angular speed of $$40$$ $$\frac{rad}{s}$$ and a radius of $$1.0$$ $$m$$, calculate the tangential speed.
